steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts Mocsary's place in historical memory with the Van Burens', who will “always be remembered” for a specific “first.” The “but” signals that despite Mocsary's accomplishments, her place has a fundamentally different quality.

steps.title: Select the word that fits the difference
steps.content:
The Van Burens' place in memory is anchored to a definite, famous achievement that “will always be remembered”—making their place fixed and certain.

Mocsary's place, grounded in broad solo travel rather than one iconic “first,” lacks that definitive anchor. “Uncertain” captures this precisely: her place in historical memory is less stable or clearly defined than the Van Burens'.

The other choices (enduring, conspicuous, deserving) all suggest lasting or positive qualities that would strengthen, not contrast with, her accomplishments.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
